name: Castillo de la Mota Astudillo Loop
name_original: Castillo de la Mota — circular trailrunning por Astudillo
description: Jog this moderate 3.6-mile (5.7 km) circular trail around the historic Castillo de la Mota in Astudillo, gaining 350 feet (107 metres).
region_intro: Jogging around Campos offers diverse landscapes, from coastal stretches to rural countryside and extensive trail networks. The region features renowned beaches like Es Trenc and Ses Covetes, providing scenic routes along the shore. Inland, the area is characterized by rural splendor, salt flats, and trails leading to viewpoints such as the Monastery of Monti-Sion. These varied environments provide a range of options for runners seeking different terrains and experiences. Best jogging routes around Campos The most popular jogging route is Sotillo de los Canónigos and Isla Dos Aguas Parks Loop, a 2.2 miles (3.6 km) trail that takes 22 minutes to complete. This easy route offers a pleasant run through urban parkland. Another top favourite among local runners is Canal de Castilla Trail, a moderate 3.7 miles (5.9 km) path. This route follows the historic Canal de Castilla, providing a flat and scenic running experience. Local runners also love the Monte El Viejo and Canal de Castilla Loop, an 8.6 miles (13.8 km) trail leading through Monte El Viejo and alongside the Canal de Castilla, often completed in about 1 hour 37 minutes. Jogging around Campos is defined by coastal paths, rural countryside, and extensive trail networks, offering options for easy, moderate, and challenging running experiences. The network includes routes suitable for various ability levels. The routes in Campos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from 9 reviews. More than 1400 runners have used komoot to explore Campos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in the Campos area?
A: The Campos area offers a wide variety of running routes, with over 120 different trails to explore. These routes cater to all levels, from easy jogs to more challenging runs.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners in Campos?
A: Yes, Campos has several easy running routes perfect for beginners. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1119458296/sotillo-de-los-canonigos-and-isla-dos-aguas-parks-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sotillo de los Canónigos and Isla Dos Aguas Parks Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.2-mile (3.6 km) trail that takes about 22 minutes to complete, offering a pleasant run through urban parkland.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running trails around Campos?
A: Running trails around Campos feature diverse terrain. You'll find flat, scenic paths along waterways like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1119449008/canal-de-castilla-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de Castilla Trail&lt;/a&gt;, as well as routes with moderate elevation changes through areas like Monte El Viejo. The region offers a mix of urban parkland, riverside paths, and natural environments.

Q: Are there any scenic running routes in Campos that offer natural beauty or points of interest?
A: Absolutely! Many routes in Campos offer scenic views and points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1119582577/monte-el-viejo-and-canal-de-castilla-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte El Viejo and Canal de Castilla Loop&lt;/a&gt; provides beautiful natural scenery. Additionally, routes along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/148691" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt; offer picturesque views of this historic waterway and its structures, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1012824" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quadruple Lock of the Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in Campos?
A: Yes, Campos offers several circular running routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1119582577/monte-el-viejo-and-canal-de-castilla-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte El Viejo and Canal de Castilla Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is an 8.6-mile (13.8 km) trail. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120117453/laguna-de-la-nava-circular-trailrunning-through-the-viewpoints-of-the-special-protection-area-for-birds" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de la Nava circular trailrunning through the viewpoints of the Special Protection Area for Birds&lt;/a&gt;, offering a unique experience.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Campos?
A: The running routes in Campos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars. Runners often praise the variety of landscapes, from urban parks to scenic canal paths, and the well-maintained trails that cater to different fitness levels. Over 1400 runners have used komoot to explore the area's diverse terrain.

Q: Are there any long-distance running trails in Campos for experienced runners?
A: For experienced runners looking for longer distances, Campos has several options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1119582577/monte-el-viejo-and-canal-de-castilla-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte El Viejo and Canal de Castilla Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.6-mile (13.8 km) trail. Another significant route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1119412318/acueducto-de-abanades-circular-trailrunning-desde-osorno-la-mayor" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Acueducto de Abánades — circular trailrunning desde Osorno la Mayor&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 11.6 km.

Q: What attractions can I see near the jogging routes in Campos?
A: Beyond the running trails, the Campos area is rich in attractions. You can explore historic sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5599889" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of La Mota of Astudillo&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6142054" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monzón de Campos Castle&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/148691" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt; itself is a major highlight, with its impressive engineering like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1012824" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quadruple Lock&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by historical landmarks in Campos?
A: Yes, several running routes in Campos offer glimpses of historical landmarks. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1119420445/castillo-de-la-mota-astudillo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de la Mota Astudillo Loop&lt;/a&gt; takes you near the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5599889" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of La Mota of Astudillo&lt;/a&gt;. The routes along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/148691" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt; also feature historical structures and scenic sections.

Q: Is there parking available near the running trails in Campos?
A: While specific parking locations for every trail are not detailed, many routes in and around Campos, especially those near towns or popular attractions like the Canal de Castilla, generally have accessible parking options. It's advisable to check the starting points of individual routes for precise parking information.

Q: Are there any routes that are particularly good for birdwatching while jogging?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120117453/laguna-de-la-nava-circular-trailrunning-through-the-viewpoints-of-the-special-protection-area-for-birds" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de la Nava circular trailrunning&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice for birdwatching. This route passes through the viewpoints of a Special Protection Area for Birds, offering unique opportunities to observe local wildlife while you run.

Q: What is a popular moderate running route in Campos?
A: A top favorite among local runners is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1119449008/canal-de-castilla-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de Castilla Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 3.7-mile (5.9 km) path. This route follows the historic Canal de Castilla, providing a flat and scenic running experience that is highly regarded.
tips: Beautiful little castle surrounded by underground mountain dwellings

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this trailrunning route?
faqs.answer: The route is designed for trail running and generally takes about 40 minutes to complete. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginner trail runners?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as moderate for trail running. While a general walking route around the castle might be easier, the uneven ground and elevation changes around the mound make it a bit more challenging for runners. Beginners with some experience in trail running should find it manageable.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: You will encounter a mix of green grassy areas and walkways. The trail circles an artificial mound, so expect some uneven ground and gentle elevation changes. It's a natural terrain route around the historic castle.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the historic Castle of La Mota of Astudillo, a medieval fortress with a rich history. You'll get continuous views of the castle from various perspectives as you run. The route also offers panoramic vistas of the surrounding plains of Palencia and you might spot nearby underground dwellings.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the "Castillo de la Mota — circular trailrunning por Astudillo" is a circular route, meaning it starts and ends in the same general area, allowing you to experience the castle from all sides.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to run this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed throughout much of the year. For the most comfortable trail running experience, consider visiting during spring or autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be warm, and winter might bring colder conditions, though the plains generally remain accessible.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Castillo de la Mota trailrunning route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details are not provided, as the trail circles the historic Castle of La Mota of Astudillo, you would typically look for parking in or near the town of Astudillo, which provides access to the castle area.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, trails in natural areas around historical sites like this are dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety and comfort of other trail users. Please clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no indication that a permit or entrance fee is required to access the trailrunning route around the Castle of La Mota of Astudillo. It appears to be freely accessible.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Calle Rampojo and the Paseo Alejandro Mieres, integrating parts of the local pathways into the circular trailrunning experience.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or services nearby?
faqs.answer: The trail is located near Astudillo, so you can expect to find local services such as cafeterias, restaurants, and possibly accommodations within the town for pre or post-run needs.
